key_insight: The paper combines detonation-gun sprayed WC-12Co and Al2O3-13TiO2 coatings with machine learning to better predict wear and friction behavior.
engineering_relevance: This supports faster coating selection and process optimization for tribological components where low wear and stable friction are critical.
method: Compared tribological performance of WC-12Co and Al2O3-13TiO2 D-gun sprayed coatings.
Used machine-learning-assisted modeling to predict wear and friction outcomes.
Framed the study for thermal-spray coating performance assessment.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a journal article in Journal of Thermal Spray Technology and notes OpenAlex indexing, but it does not provide dataset size or validation details.
watch_item: Prediction utility may be limited without clear reporting of feature set, training data, and external validation across deposition conditions.
paper_title: Tribological Performance and Machine Learning-Assisted Prediction of Wear and Friction in D-Gun Sprayed WC-12Co and Al2O3-13TiO2 Coatings
